<item>
      <title>Blood on the Dance Floor: Assassination, Invasion, and a King Undone by Love — Vijayanagara Empire Chapter 3</title>
      <description>In this chapter, we witness the brutal succession struggles of the Bahmani Sultanate — where boy-kings are blinded within weeks of taking the throne — and the dramatic assassination of Bukka II's son by a Bahmani spy named Cauzi Serauje, who infiltrated the Hindu camp disguised as a musician during a night entertainment, charmed the prince with his playing, then plunged daggers into his breast during a knife dance. The ensuing chaos allowed Sultan Firuz Shah to cross the flooded Krishna River and rout the Vijayanagar army, leading to a humiliating peace treaty. We then follow the reckless King Deva Raya I, whose obsessive desire for a farmer's beautiful daughter named Pertal — a girl who courageously refused his proposal — drove him to launch a military raid into Bahmani territory, sparking a catastrophic war that ended with the unprecedented disgrace of giving a Hindu princess in marriage to a Muslim sultan. Based on A Forgotten Empire (Vijayanagara) by Robert Sewell (1900).</description>
      <enclosure url="https://drive.google.com/uc?export=download&id=1WyXoUAla4pj3YKvxy2biachMEIBHziQ_" type="audio/mpeg" length="40572385" />
